top_stories.label: Top Stories
top_stories.ai_summary: The world is balanced between fragile peace moves and fresh violence. Trump says an Iran deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz is 'largely negotiated,' even as Russia pounded Kyiv with one of the biggest barrages of the war. Beijing dominated diplomacy with back-to-back Trump and Putin summits sketching rival visions of a multipolar order, while Africa scrambles to contain a deadly new Ebola outbreak.

stories.headline: Russia pounds Kyiv with 90 missiles and 600 drones, fires rare Oreshnik in one of war's largest attacks
stories.summary: Russia launched its biggest aerial assault on Kyiv in a year overnight, killing at least four people, damaging about 30 residential buildings, and firing a nuclear-capable Oreshnik hypersonic missile at Bila Tserkva. Zelensky demanded 'consequences,' and the strike undercuts Putin's recent claim the war is 'coming to an end.' It signals Moscow is escalating pressure on civilians even as peace talks are floated.

stories.headline: Trump says Iran deal to reopen Strait of Hormuz is 'largely negotiated,' announcement expected soon
stories.summary: President Trump said a framework deal with Iran, including a 60-day ceasefire and the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, has been mostly agreed and could be unveiled imminently. Iran would clear mines and resume oil exports while the U.S. lifts its blockade and eases some sanctions, with nuclear talks to follow. Big disputes remain over enrichment and sanctions timing, but the move would defuse the world's most dangerous current chokepoint.

stories.headline: Putin and Xi sign 47-page 'multipolar world' declaration in Beijing, days after Trump-Xi summit
stories.summary: Russia and China inked roughly 40 cooperation deals and a sweeping joint declaration pushing back against U.S. 'hegemony' and calling for a 'polycentric' international order. The pact, renewing their 2001 friendship treaty, lands just days after Trump's own Beijing summit with Xi produced trade promises but no clear strategic alignment. It cements the Moscow-Beijing axis as a counterweight to Washington.

stories.headline: Trump-Xi Beijing summit ends in 'strategic stability' pact but sides disagree on what they agreed
stories.summary: Trump and Xi emerged from a two-day Beijing summit pledging a new vision of 'strategic stability,' a $30 billion-each tariff-reduction framework, 200 Boeing jet orders and $17 billion a year in U.S. farm purchases. But Washington touted the trade wins while Beijing emphasized Taiwan warnings, with neither side confirming the other's account. The deliberate ambiguity buys time but leaves the core U.S.-China rivalry unresolved.

stories.headline: WHO declares Ebola outbreak in DRC and Uganda a global health emergency as deaths near 220
stories.summary: An outbreak of the Bundibugyo strain of Ebola has spread across three eastern DRC provinces with cases now reported in Kampala, prompting the WHO to declare a Public Health Emergency of International Concern. As of May 23 there were 968 suspected cases and at least 216 deaths, and no approved vaccine or treatment exists for this strain. Health agencies are racing to contain spillover into neighbouring countries.

stories.headline: Nearly 2 million Muslims begin Hajj in Mecca under shadow of Iran war and tightened security
stories.summary: Saudi Arabia is hosting one of its largest Hajj seasons ever, with more than 1.5 million foreign pilgrims already arrived and totals expected to near 1.8 million. The pilgrimage is unfolding against a fragile Iran-war ceasefire, with the kingdom deploying drones, AI crowd-monitoring and a streamlined Nusuk digital platform. Smooth passage matters both spiritually and as a stability signal for the wider region.

stories.headline: China launches Shenzhou-23 with Hong Kong's first astronaut and a planned yearlong stay in orbit
stories.summary: China sent three astronauts to its Tiangong space station on Sunday, including Lai Ka-ying, the first crew member from Hong Kong, with one team member set to spend a full year in orbit studying long-duration human spaceflight. The mission will conduct dozens of experiments and rotate out the Shenzhou-21 crew. It underscores Beijing's accelerating push to rival U.S. dominance in human spaceflight.

singapore.label: Singapore
singapore.ai_summary: Singapore's economy roared ahead with surprise 6% Q1 growth on AI demand, even as MTI kept the full-year forecast at 2–4% citing Middle East energy risks. April inflation cooled to 1.8%, but officials warn the Iran conflict could lift electricity bills. Major job stories dominated: StanChart's CEO apologised for calling staff 'lower-value human capital' as it cuts 7,800 roles, and H&M is shifting its SEA HQ to Kuala Lumpur, axing about 78 Singapore jobs.

stories.headline: Singapore's Q1 GDP grows 6%, blowing past 4.6% advance estimate on AI demand
stories.summary: The Ministry of Trade and Industry confirmed today that the economy expanded 6% year-on-year in Q1 2026, well above its earlier 4.6% flash estimate, with manufacturing up 7.9% and wholesale trade up 11.7%. Strong AI-related demand drove electronics, precision engineering and machinery. MTI is keeping its full-year forecast at 2–4% because of energy disruptions linked to the Middle East conflict.

stories.headline: Singapore keeps 2026 growth forecast at 2–4% despite strong Q1, citing Iran-war risks
stories.summary: Even after the upbeat Q1 print, MTI kept its 2026 GDP forecast unchanged at 2–4% on Monday, warning that crude oil shortages and price spikes from the US–Israel–Iran conflict are already hurting fuels and chemicals trade. Headline inflation for April came in lower than expected at 1.8%. Officials say the second half of 2026 will be much softer than the first.

stories.headline: StanChart CEO apologises for 'lower-value human capital' jibe as AI cuts 7,800 jobs
stories.summary: Standard Chartered chief Bill Winters issued a LinkedIn apology after describing redundant staff as 'lower-value human capital' at a Hong Kong investor event. The bank is cutting more than 15% of its support workforce, around 7,800 jobs, by 2030 as it accelerates AI adoption. Former president Halimah Yacob called the wording 'demeaning' and urged the bank to retrench workers humanely.

stories.headline: H&M moving SEA headquarters to Kuala Lumpur, around 78 Singapore jobs affected
stories.summary: H&M is relocating its Southeast Asia regional HQ from Singapore to Kuala Lumpur, with employees told that interviews and evaluations will run from May 25 to June 19. About 78 of 256 regional support roles, mostly in Singapore, will be removed by July 1. Store-level operations and retail staff are unaffected.

stories.headline: 12 young Singaporeans to be charged this week over fraudulent SIM card syndicate
stories.summary: Eight men and four women aged 17 to 28 will be charged in court between May 25 and 29 for allegedly handing over postpaid SIM cards registered in their names to a criminal syndicate. Police say they were paid just S$10 to S$20 per SIM card. Scam mules who supply SIM cards or Singpass credentials can now face discretionary caning of up to 12 strokes.

stories.headline: NEA: Thundery showers to keep hitting Singapore on most days in late May
stories.summary: The National Environment Agency says short-duration thundery showers are expected over parts of the island on most afternoons for the rest of May, with Sumatra squalls possible in the early morning on a few days. Daily highs will hit 33–35 °C, occasionally above 35 °C. Tampines already saw 114.6mm of rain in a single day earlier this month, the heaviest of the fortnight.

stories.headline: COE prices: Cat B SUVs hit S$129,501, large cars Cat E close at S$127,700
stories.summary: Singapore's most recent COE bidding closed Category A at S$124,229, Category B at S$129,501 (up S$3,265), Category C (goods vehicles) at S$87,479, motorcycles at S$9,452 and Open Category E at S$127,700. LTA has trimmed Cat A supply by 2% and boosted Cat B by 7% for the May–July quota. Up to 20,000 extra COEs are being progressively injected to ease prices.

stories.headline: HDB confirms June BTO launch of 6,900 flats across five towns
stories.summary: HDB has confirmed the next BTO exercise in June 2026 will offer about 6,900 flats across seven projects in Ang Mo Kio, Bishan, Bukit Merah, Sembawang and Woodlands. More than 4,000 of this year's 19,600 BTO flats will be Shorter Waiting Time units of under three years. The mix spans Standard, Plus and Prime flats.

wars.label: Wars & Conflicts
wars.ai_summary: All three theatres remain hot despite parallel diplomatic tracks. Russia escalated overnight on May 24 with a 600-drone/90-missile barrage on Kyiv using a hypersonic Oreshnik, even as Trump–Putin ceasefire talks stagnate. The US–Iran war is closest to de-escalation: Trump said May 23–24 the Hormuz reopening deal is 'largely negotiated,' though Tehran disputes terms. Israel keeps striking Lebanon (six killed May 24) and Gaza despite October 2025 truces.

theatres.name: Russia–Ukraine War
theatres.status: Active — heavy strikes; ceasefire talks stagnated after May 9–11 Victory Day truce

timeline.event: Russia launches full-scale invasion of Ukraine, beginning a war that has now stretched into its fifth year.
Trump announces a three-day Victory Day ceasefire agreed by Moscow and Kyiv, including an exchange of 1,000 prisoners each.
Three-day ceasefire begins; Putin says he believes the war 'is coming to an end' and floats direct talks with Zelensky.
Ukraine counters Putin's short truce by proposing a long-term ceasefire; Zelensky says Kyiv wants a 30-day cessation, not a symbolic pause.
Secretary of State Marco Rubio publicly concedes US mediation 'has stagnated'; analysts accuse Moscow of 'simulating diplomacy' to mask battlefield losses.
Ukrainian drones strike a Russian drone-pilot training camp in occupied Snizhne, Donetsk; Kyiv says at least 65 cadets and an instructor were killed.
Putin orders retaliation after blaming Ukraine for a drone strike on a college in occupied Starobilsk; Kyiv denies targeting the site.

Russia launches one of the war's largest combined attacks — 600 drones and 90 missiles, including a hypersonic Oreshnik — on Kyiv, killing at least four and injuring around 100; damage reported in every district.
Recovery efforts continue across Kyiv; Zelensky says about 30 residential buildings were damaged or destroyed and renews calls for tougher Western sanctions and air defences.

theatres.name: US/Israel–Iran Conflict (Strait of Hormuz)
theatres.status: Fragile ceasefire — Hormuz-reopening deal 'largely negotiated' but unsigned

timeline.event: US and Israel launch 'Operation Epic Fury,' striking Iranian military and leadership sites; Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and several family members are killed in Tehran.
Iran retaliates with missile and drone barrages on Israel, US bases and Gulf allies, and closes the Strait of Hormuz, choking ~25% of global seaborne oil.
Mojtaba Khamenei is designated Iran's new supreme leader, consolidating hardline control during the war.
A conditional US–Iran ceasefire takes hold, punctuated by repeated skirmishes over Hormuz access.
Iran declares it is re-closing the Strait of Hormuz, citing US 'breaches of trust' during the truce.
Trump launches 'Operation Project Freedom,' a US Navy mission to escort merchant ships out of the Gulf; Iran calls it a ceasefire violation.
Trump pauses Project Freedom citing 'great progress' toward a wider agreement with Tehran.
Trump says on social media that an Iran deal reopening Hormuz is 'largely negotiated' and will be announced shortly.

Reports detail a draft framework: 60-day ceasefire extension, Iranian de-mining of Hormuz, resumed Iranian oil sales and parallel nuclear talks; Iran's Fars agency disputes terms, insisting the strait stays 'under Iran's management.'
Trump tells negotiators 'not to rush' a deal, saying 'time is on our side'; shipping through Hormuz still at roughly 5% of pre-war levels.

theatres.name: Israel–Gaza/Lebanon
theatres.status: Fragile truces fraying — daily Israeli strikes in Lebanon; Gaza Phase II stalled

timeline.event: Hamas-led attacks on southern Israel kill ~1,200 and take ~250 hostages, igniting the Gaza war and a parallel Hezbollah–Israel front in Lebanon.
Israel and Hamas sign Phase One of Trump's 20-point Gaza peace plan in Sharm el-Sheikh; ceasefire takes effect October 10 with hostage-for-prisoner exchange.
A US-brokered Israel–Lebanon ceasefire takes effect after a renewed Lebanon war; intended as a 10-day truce to enable longer-term talks.
Israel issues forced evacuation orders across southern Lebanon, signalling a sharp escalation despite the ceasefire.
Israel threatens to resume the Gaza war to force Hamas disarmament as the October truce frays; over 800 Palestinians reported killed since the ceasefire began.
Israeli strikes in Gaza City's Rimal kill Hamas Gaza chief Izz al-Din al-Haddad; the Israel–Lebanon truce is extended 45 days after a third round of Washington talks.
At least 22 people, including women and children, killed by Israeli airstrikes on Deir Qanoun an-Naher, Nabatieh and Kfar Sir in southern Lebanon.
UN Board of Peace envoy Nickolay Mladenov warns the UN Security Council that Gaza's deteriorating status quo risks becoming 'permanent'; Hamas rejects Phase II disarmament demands.
Six Lebanese paramedics killed in two separate Israeli strikes in Hanaway and Deir Qanoun an-Naher, drawing international condemnation.
Israeli strikes kill at least six in southern Lebanon (including a paramedic) as the IDF issues 16 fresh evacuation orders; Hezbollah claims attacks on Israeli positions.
Diplomats prepare for a May 29 Israel–Lebanon–US military coordination meeting at the Pentagon ahead of a fourth round of peace talks on June 2–3.

hantavirus.label: Hantavirus Outbreak
hantavirus.ai_summary: Two major outbreaks dominate the global health picture: the Andes hantavirus cluster from the MV Hondius cruise ship (12 cases, 3 deaths across 12 countries) and a fast-growing Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak in DRC and Uganda, now a WHO public health emergency of international concern. Singapore's two exposed residents have tested negative. Public risk in Singapore and the US remains low, but travellers should watch screening and respiratory symptoms.

stories.headline: ECDC: Andes hantavirus outbreak on MV Hondius rises to 12 cases, 3 deaths
stories.summary: As of 24 May, the cruise-ship cluster has 12 cases (10 confirmed, 2 probable) and 3 deaths — one new case since the prior update. The ship is docked in Rotterdam undergoing sanitation, and ECDC says the risk to the general EU/EEA population remains very low. Watch for any secondary cases among former passengers still under monitoring in 12 countries.

stories.headline: WHO multi-country Disease Outbreak News: hantavirus cluster linked to cruise ship travel
stories.summary: WHO's DON entry confirms Andes virus as the cause of the MV Hondius cluster, the only hantavirus known to spread person-to-person. Former passengers are being followed in Australia, Canada, France, Germany, the Netherlands, Singapore, the US and others. WHO assesses global public health risk as low but cites the wide geographic dispersion of contacts.

stories.headline: CDC update: 18 US passengers held at Nebraska Quarantine Facility through May 31
stories.summary: CDC says 18 repatriated US passengers from MV Hondius will remain at the Nebraska Quarantine Facility through May 31, 2026. No confirmed Andes virus cases have been reported in the US and CDC continues to rate the risk to the American public as extremely low. The agency has issued HAN advisories to clinicians on testing.

stories.headline: Singapore CDA: both MV Hondius residents test negative for hantavirus
stories.summary: Singapore's Communicable Diseases Agency confirmed both Singapore residents who were on the MV Hondius have tested negative for Andes virus, including by the National Public Health Laboratory. They remain in 30-day quarantine and will be retested before release, then phone-monitored to day 45 (the maximum incubation period). Community risk in Singapore is assessed as low.

stories.headline: WHO declares Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak in DRC and Uganda a PHEIC
stories.summary: On 16 May WHO declared the Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak a public health emergency of international concern. As of 21 May, DRC reports 746 suspected cases and 176 deaths, with 85 confirmed cases and 10 deaths across both countries (two cases in Kampala, Uganda). The outbreak has spread from Ituri into North and South Kivu — watch for further geographic expansion.

stories.headline: CDC and DHS roll out enhanced US travel screening for Ebola
stories.summary: On 18 May, CDC and the Department of Homeland Security announced enhanced entry screening, travel restrictions and public-health measures targeting travellers from affected parts of East and Central Africa. The action follows the rapid expansion of the Bundibugyo outbreak. Travellers from the region should expect extra screening at US ports of entry.

stories.headline: US measles cases approach 2,000, elimination status at risk
stories.summary: CDC data show roughly 1,952 confirmed measles cases across 33 US jurisdictions in 2026, with Utah alone at 441 and 29 active outbreaks; 93% of cases are outbreak-linked. Analysts say the US is highly likely to lose its measles elimination status this autumn. CDC warns of more spread through summer travel — unvaccinated travellers are the main risk group.

stories.headline: WHO flags recombinant mpox virus with clade Ib and IIb elements
stories.summary: A WHO DON describes a recombinant MPXV carrying genetic material from both clades Ib and IIb, with cases detected in the UK and India. Clade I continues to drive substantial outbreaks in DRC, Uganda, Burundi, Kenya and Madagascar, and EU/EEA countries are still reporting clade I in men who have sex with men without travel history. Sexual contact remains the main amplifier.

stories.headline: WHO logs first 2026 H5N1 child fatality in Bangladesh; human-to-human risk still low
stories.summary: WHO has tracked 13 avian and swine influenza cases in people in Q1 2026, including a previously unreported fatal H5N1 case in a child from Bangladesh. None of the close contacts of the 13 patients fell ill and sustained human-to-human transmission has not been observed. CDC says US public risk is low but H5N1 is now in more US dairy herds than ever — watch farm-worker exposures.

cybersecurity.label: CyberSecurity
cybersecurity.ai_summary: This cycle is dominated by mass-exploitation events with confirmed in-the-wild attacks. Drupal's critical SQL injection (CVE-2026-9082) is already being hit at scale, the Laravel-Lang Composer packages were hijacked to steal developer credentials across 700+ versions, and a maximum-severity LiteSpeed cPanel flaw (CVSS 10.0) is letting attackers gain root. Defenders should prioritise patching these alongside Ubiquiti UniFi OS and Trend Micro Apex One zero-days that CISA has added to KEV.

stories.headline: Drupal Core SQL injection (CVE-2026-9082) actively exploited, added to CISA KEV
stories.summary: A highly critical SQL injection flaw in Drupal Core's PostgreSQL EntityQuery handler is being exploited in the wild, with Imperva observing 15,000+ attack attempts against nearly 6,000 sites across 65 countries. Drupal rates it Highly Critical and CISA added it to the KEV catalog. Only sites running PostgreSQL are affected; fixes are available in 11.3.10, 11.2.12, 11.1.10, 10.6.9, 10.5.10 and 10.4.10.

stories.headline: Laravel-Lang PHP packages hijacked to deliver cross-platform credential stealer
stories.summary: On May 22–23 an attacker with push access to the Laravel-Lang GitHub organisation rewrote every git tag across multiple popular Composer packages, with up to 700 historical versions backdoored. The injected ~5,900-line PHP stealer harvests cloud, Kubernetes, Vault, Git, CI/CD, SSH, browser, wallet and .env secrets across Linux, macOS and Windows, exfiltrating AES-256-encrypted data. Packagist has unlisted the affected packages.

stories.headline: LiteSpeed cPanel plugin zero-day (CVE-2026-48172) exploited for root access
stories.summary: A CVSS 10.0 incorrect-privilege-assignment flaw in the LiteSpeed User-End cPanel Plugin lets any logged-in cPanel user run arbitrary scripts as root via a single malformed call to the lsws.redisAble JSON-API endpoint. Active exploitation is confirmed and automated scanning is widespread. Patched in plugin 2.4.5 / WHM plugin 5.3.1.0 (cPanel plugin 2.4.7+).

stories.headline: Ubiquiti patches three maximum-severity UniFi OS vulnerabilities
stories.summary: Ubiquiti released fixes for five UniFi OS flaws, three of which are CVSS 10.0 (CVE-2026-34908, -34909, -34910) covering unauthorised system changes, path traversal and unauthenticated command injection. Affected products include UDM, UDM-Pro, UCG, UNVR and UniFi OS Server, with Censys tracking nearly 100,000 internet-exposed endpoints. No active exploitation yet, but admins should update to UniFi OS 5.1.12+ / Server 5.0.8+ immediately.

stories.headline: Trend Micro Apex One zero-day (CVE-2026-34926) exploited, added to CISA KEV
stories.summary: Trend Micro patched a directory-traversal flaw in on-premise Apex One that allows an attacker with server admin credentials to modify a key table and push malicious code to managed agents. Trend Micro has observed at least one in-the-wild exploitation attempt and CISA added the bug to the KEV catalog, giving federal agencies until June 4 to remediate. Affects Apex One 2019 on-premise builds below 17079.

stories.headline: Ghost CMS SQL injection (CVE-2026-26980) abused in 700-site ClickFix campaign
stories.summary: XLab researchers found attackers exploiting an unauthenticated SQL injection in the Ghost CMS Content API to steal admin keys and inject malicious JavaScript into articles on 700+ domains, including Harvard, Oxford, Auburn University and DuckDuckGo. Visitors are funneled to a fake Cloudflare ClickFix page that tricks them into pasting commands that deploy DLL loaders, JS droppers and an Electron-based malware sample. A patch has existed since February but many sites remain unpatched.

stories.headline: Linux kernel 'Copy Fail' root flaw (CVE-2026-31431) added to CISA KEV
stories.summary: A nine-year-old logic bug in the Linux kernel's authentication cryptographic template, dubbed Copy Fail, lets any local user become root via a 732-byte Python exploit that triggers a 4-byte page-cache overwrite. It affects virtually every Linux distribution including Ubuntu 24.04, RHEL 10.1, Amazon Linux 2023, SUSE 16, Debian and Fedora. CISA has confirmed in-the-wild exploitation and added it to KEV; fixes are in kernels 6.18.22, 6.19.12 and 7.0.

stories.headline: Microsoft Exchange Server zero-day (CVE-2026-42897) exploited via crafted email
stories.summary: Microsoft confirmed exploitation of an on-prem Exchange Server spoofing/XSS flaw that can be triggered by a specially crafted email, allowing attackers to act in the context of the victim. The bug affects Exchange Server Subscription Edition, 2016 and 2019, and CISA added it to KEV on May 15. Admins are urged to apply the May security update immediately.
